Because none of the CAPEX is sustainable. Data centers are plagued by delay, probably that a majority of microsoft ai chips are sitting in warehouses, not a single company can give a ROI to their AI spendings. SPV financing scheme needs stellar liquidity from private credit, and that liquidity has taken a hit in the last few months. Company are stopping tokenmaxxing as costs were way higher than expected. Core Nvda buyers like coreweave are going through a bond crisis with rates around 12% and 35B in expected Capex this year, which in my opinion they have no way to find the cash. Us power grid is not following expected data center buildout. Chinese competition has closen the gap and is only 4-6 month behind leading US models for around 1/20th of the cost. Investors are worried that Space x ipo might be weaker than expected setting a bad precedent for future AI IPO, and these ipo needs to be stellar otherwise OpenAI and Anthropic will have difficulty to respect spending commitment. Credit default swaps of major hyperscaler have seen an increase in the last few month, this will increase the cost of borrowing of these company. I can't seem to find the rational on why going long other

First close below 50WMA since April low, soft support all the way down to $640 on QQQ. Grats bears, looks like this is it.

Yeah NDX sounds way better for this kind of stuff. I always wondered why people stick with QQQ when you have these weird expiration rules that can mess up your timing. RH really loves to screw people over with their random restrictions - like they're protecting us from making money or something

  
The UI is pretty but not worth losing trades like that, especially when market is moving fast at end of day

We're gyrating around and going through some motions but this is a textbook price correction after an insane-o run up in a narrow theme.

QQQ (and the Nasdaq is driving this) has to go down to about 670 to fill a couple gaps which is coincidentally about a 10% drop from the top and also connects with the first major moving averages on a 1 day interval chart.

Y'all can say this is hocus pocus technical bullshit but odds are it plays out something like this.  

There is no major change in the market outlook or key themes just a need to pull back on prices between earnings seasons.
If you liked this almost 10% pullback on QQQ you’ll love it by August when it’s down 70% more.
range bound to the cents now because QQQ algos don't want it to drop sub 696.

top_bearish_posts.text:
The semi sell-off has to do with a report that SemiAnalysis sent out yesterday that claims that CPO rollout will be delayed to 2028/29.

But multiple pushbacks on this narrative:

https://x.com/aleabitoreddit/status/2064521404859719681?s=46

They didn't want a bunch of shares getting dumped at once so they chose to stagger it more. The IPO shares only represent about 4% of the total shares which is far lower than most IPOs. The other 96% are part of the lockup. 


It's not some big conspiracy like some people are making it out to be. Having too many shares unlock at once can cause a big selloff. About 10-20% of IPOs don't follow the standard 180 lockup. It can be negotiated with the underwriters.
Fidelity says they'll ban you from future IPOs if you didn't hold for 15 days. Lol

top_bullish_posts.text:
If that happens then yeah that's bad news bears for Nvidia, that's also bad news bears for literally everything else, because at that point, you could arguably use those quantized agents to create an undeniably better Coca Cola formula and run an undeniably better Coca Cola ad campaign. The "models get better" is doing a lot of heavy lifting here because;

Everything we've seen so far points to MORE compute not less. You want a model to output better more precise fact checked outputs? That needs more compute so you can run multiple models for longer.

Need your model to look through all your data and draw conclusions? More tokens, more compute.

Need your model to take actions on your behalf? Even more tokens.

Need your model to generate legible code (not talking about efficiency, I'm talking about inefficiency for the sake of legibility) then the model ends up using more tokens and compute.

The only things that save these skyrocketing compute costs are

A. An abundance of compute.  
B. ASI level efficiency gains. 

The models are already "getting better" every day. Problem is very 10% better you make them it costs like 200-500% more tokens. And those numbers are only getting mor

top_bullish_posts.text:
Been following some of these defense names for while now and this data is pretty solid. LDOS especially caught my attention since those congressional selling patterns stopping is usually good indicator something brewing. The timing with all these contracts dropping June 8th definitely not coincidence.

  
One thing I learned from tracking defense stocks is that mid cap ones like these tend to have more volatile moves when contracts hit compared to the big boys. Your backtesting confirms what I've seen - LMT and RTX barely move anymore on contract news since everyone expects them to win everything anyway. But these smaller players can really pop when they land something significant.

  
PSN looks interesting even without the political buying signals - that T+90 performance in your data is pretty compelling. BWXT worries me bit though with that low hit rate on longer timeframe, might be more of quick swing play. Defense sector overall has been getting lot of tailwinds lately so timing seems right for this kind of strategy.
